comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Live triage evidence (rebar-debug, with the merged fix on origin/main). Ran `rebar bridge-fsck` against the real store: ALL actionable/alerting drift classes are now EMPTY — would_terminal=0, local_gone=0, retired_overlap=0, dangling=0, unbound_jira=0. The 176 previously-false 'dangling' entries are now correctly in the non-alerting informational bucket absent_in_window_unprobed=176 (alive Done issues aged out of the windowed snapshot; per ADR 0028 §1 absence != deletion). Triage result: NO actionable drift to remediate; the 176 are working-as-designed. The literal AC 'empty binding_drift section' was proven unsatisfiable under the OLD conflation (window-absence counted as dangling grows unbounded); with the fix the ALERTING binding_drift is empty and only the informational bucket is populated.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: FORCE_CLOSE: close gate(s) bypassed by user approval — no completion/signature attestation was signed. Reason: "Fixed via Gerrit 848 (ADR 0028): window-absence no longer flagged dangling. Remaining ACs assumed the 163 dangling were real; the fix proves them false positives. Live bridge-fsck: 0 actionable drift (176 correctly informational). No triage to perform; literal ACs unsatisfiable-by-correct-design. Evidence on ticket.". Session: 5e1736fd-6a46-425f-96e3-3460f9fcb4f7.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Releasing back to open: this is a bot-authored binding-drift ALERT ticket whose closure is owned by the reconcile-bridge canary's close-on-recovery automation, not manual close. The code fix is merged (Gerrit 848) and the canary --class break is fixed (Gerrit 853). Binding drift has recovered (live bridge-fsck: 0 actionable drift). Returning to 'open' so the now-fixed canary auto-closes this alert on its next recovery pass (the correct lifecycle), rather than force-closing manually.

compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps

Run `rebar bridge-fsck` — the offline binding-drift arm (epic 3006-e198,
the convergence parity oracle) reports a non-empty `binding_drift` section.

## Expected vs Actual

- **Expected:** `binding_drift` is empty (every bound pair converged).
- **Actual:** dangling=8
- **Detected at:** 2026-07-14T16:13:45Z
- **Canary run:** https://github.com/navapbc/rebar/actions/runs/29348602347

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Each reported drift is triaged (would_terminal → local archive/delete
      to propagate; dangling → confirmed 404 GC; unbound_jira → adopt or
      archive the Jira-native issue; local_gone → investigate).
- [ ] `rebar bridge-fsck` reports an empty `binding_drift` section.

This ticket auto-closes when bridge-fsck next reports zero binding drift.
compiled_state.title: [binding-drift] bridge-fsck found 8 unhealed binding drift(s)
